title: Options-implied Monte Carlo calibration
hypothesis: Monte Carlo simulations produce more market-consistent projections when calibrated with forward-looking options data (IV skew, put/call ratio, IV rank, term structure) rather than relying solely on backward-looking GARCH volatility estimates.
what_i_noticed: Options intelligence service already computes IV skew (1.566 SPY), P/C ratio (1.987), IV rank (74.4%) — all forward-looking signals never fed into MC engine
MC currently relies on GARCH (backward-looking) for vol and ML crash prob for jumps — missing the market's own consensus on future risk
SPY options show bearish positioning (P/C ~2.0, IV rank 74%) but this isn't reflected in the MC crash frequency
The stock_analyzer runs per-ticker MC without any options-implied adjustments despite options data being 1 API call away
what_i_did: Created backend/services/options_calibrator.py — bridges options intelligence output into MC-compatible parameters
Calibrator extracts: implied vol (GARCH+IV blend), jump frequency multiplier (from P/C + IV rank), jump magnitude adjustment (from IV skew), vol mean-reversion speed (from term structure)
Wired calibrator into stock_analyzer.py — if options data is available and confidence > 0.2, MC parameters are adjusted
Added options_calibration config section to config.py with tunable thresholds
All adjustments scaled by confidence (partial data = partial effect), bounded to prevent extreme distortion
Wrote 30 unit tests covering null cases, IV blending, jump freq, jump magnitude, vol kappa, confidence, integration
tests_status: 30 new tests passing, all 93 MC + signal tests passing, all 42 drift/options/edge tests passing

results.before: MC for individual stocks uses only GARCH vol + ML crash prob. Options data computed but not fed back into projections.
results.after: MC blends ATM IV (35% weight) with GARCH vol, adjusts crash frequency based on P/C ratio and IV rank, adjusts crash magnitude based on IV skew, adjusts vol mean-reversion based on term structure. All bounded and confidence-gated.
results.example_bearish: SPY with P/C=2.5, IV_rank=90, skew=1.8 → jump_freq_mult=1.45, jump_mag_adj=-0.03, vol higher
results.example_bullish: AAPL with P/C=0.6, IV_rank=15, skew=0.95 → jump_freq_mult=0.75, vol slightly lower

design_decisions: 35% IV blend weight — IV is noisy (bid-ask spread, liquidity) but more forward-looking; GARCH is stable but lagging
Confidence-gated: low-volume/illiquid tickers get minimal adjustment. Threshold at 0.2 confidence to engage.
Non-blocking: wrapped in try/except — if options fetch fails, MC runs exactly as before
Narrow exception types (ImportError, KeyError, TypeError, ValueError, AttributeError) to satisfy code quality test
self_critique: Does not yet wire into run_monte_carlo (S&P 500 market-level MC) — only per-stock. VIX term structure could improve market-level too.
Options data fetch adds latency to analyze_stock — could be parallelized or cached.
The 35% IV blend weight is a reasonable default but not empirically tuned — would benefit from backtest validation.
No integration test with real options data (would require network call) — unit tests use synthetic data.
next_steps: Wire options calibration into S&P 500 run_monte_carlo (simulation router) using VIX term structure
Add options calibration caching (options data changes slowly, ~1hr TTL is fine)
Backtest: compare MC accuracy with vs without options calibration on historical data
Consider using options-implied probability density (Breeden-Litzenberger) for direct MC calibration
